(The official menu may briefly show the: (The official menu may briefly show the wrong model/effort until the first turn — this panel’s model · effort badges are the real state.)
Stopped at task {0} — see the message a: Stopped at task {0} — see the message above.
Wave 1 of {0} opened — the rest will fo: Wave 1 of {0} opened — the rest will follow (auto) or wait for ▶ (manual).
{0} could not be identified (no session: {0} could not be identified (no session file) — model/effort mismatch badge unavailable for those.
{0} not identified yet — they will link: {0} not identified yet — they will link themselves once started, or use “Link…” in the group.
A task in wave {0} will not finish on it: A task in wave {0} will not finish on its own (interrupted, or its tab was closed before anything was sent) — auto advance is suspended. Use ▶ to force wave {1}.
Add a conversation to this group: Add a conversation to this group
Add an existing conversation to this gro: Add an existing conversation to this group
Advance to the next wave automatically o: Advance to the next wave automatically once this one is fully done
Claude Convs plays its own notification: Claude Convs plays its own notification sound. VS Code also has an accessibility sound enabled for chat responses / questions — turn those off to avoid hearing both?
Claude Convs: could not open the convers: Claude Convs: could not open the conversation with its prompt ({0}). An empty conversation was opened and the prompt is in your clipboard — paste it there.
Claude Convs: hooks installed. Reload th: Claude Convs: hooks installed. Reload the window for the panel to pick up live conversation state.
Claude Convs: install.ps1 not found in t: Claude Convs: install.ps1 not found in the extension folder.
Claude Convs: no ungrouped conversation: Claude Convs: no ungrouped conversation to pick from.
Dissolve this group (conversations are k: Dissolve this group (conversations are kept, nothing is closed)
Dissolve “{0}”?\n\nThe {1} conversation(:
Dissolve “{0}”?

The {1} conversation(s) stay exactly as they are — open tabs are not closed and nothing is interrupted. Only the grouping disappears.
Finished — its tab has been closed.
Forget which conversation this batch cam: Forget which conversation this batch came from (the conversation itself is kept)
Interrupted — not in the panel list righ: Interrupted — not in the panel list right now.
Its process is gone and it never reached: Its process is gone and it never reached the end of a turn.
Link this task to an existing conversati: Link this task to an existing conversation
Not linked to a conversation yet.
Notification sounds are on, but the Clau: Notification sounds are on, but the Claude Code hooks aren't installed — without them, conversations never leave the "idle" state, so the sound will never actually play. Install the hooks now?
Only advance to the next wave when I cli: Only advance to the next wave when I click ▶
Open several conversations at once, each: Open several conversations at once, each with its own prompt, model and effort.
Open, but not in the panel list right no: Open, but not in the panel list right now.
Prompt for this conversation — or paste: Prompt for this conversation — or paste a /handoffs block
Queued — opens when this wave starts.
Remove from the group (the conversation: Remove from the group (the conversation itself is kept)
Running, but not in the panel list right: Running, but not in the panel list right now.
Set the conversation this batch came fro: Set the conversation this batch came from
Tab open with the prompt inserted — pres: Tab open with the prompt inserted — press Enter to start it.
The tab was closed before anything was s: The tab was closed before anything was sent.
This conversation is finished — close it: This conversation is finished — close its tab
This will deploy Claude Code hooks so th: This will deploy Claude Code hooks so the panel can show live conversation state (busy/waiting/done) instead of idle only. It writes to:
Make Claude end its handoffs with this b: Make Claude end its handoffs with this block — copy an instruction for your CLAUDE.md.
Dismiss this tip — the ? above brings it: Dismiss this tip — the ? above brings it back
Waiting for you, but not in the panel li: Waiting for you, but not in the panel list right now.
claude-convs block not recognized: {0} —: claude-convs block not recognized: {0} — kept as a plain prompt.
claude-convs block recognized — {0} task: claude-convs block recognized — {0} task(s) prefilled (model, effort, waves).
group: only allowed at the top of the fi: group: only allowed at the top of the first section
session: only allowed at the top of the: session: only allowed at the top of the first section
stale — no activity for a while: stale — no activity for a while
wave {0}: {1}/{2} done — wave {3} opens: wave {0}: {1}/{2} done — wave {3} opens automatically once this one is complete.
{0} set in claudeCode.environmentVariabl: {0} set in claudeCode.environmentVariables — that setting is applied after ours, so it would override any choice made here. Model/effort selectors are disabled; remove it from VS Code settings to pick per-conversation values.
{0} tab closed before sending — reopen i: {0} tab closed before sending — reopen it and press Enter to link it to a conversation.
{0} tabs closed before sending — reopen: {0} tabs closed before sending — reopen them and press Enter to link them to a conversation.
